channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Fall of Assad regime a 'moment of historic opportunity' for Syrian people, Biden says
item.description: President Biden said the U.S. will support Syria's neighboring nations and help bolster stability in the region after Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad fled.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Assad's fall in Syria is a 'fundamental act of justice,' but also 'a moment of risk,' Biden says
item.description: President Joe Biden says the sudden collapse of the Syrian government under Bashar Assad is a “fundamental act of justice” after decades of repression, but that it's “a moment of risk and uncertainty” for the Mideast.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Netanyahu hails 'historic' fall of Bashar Assad in Syria, credits Israeli attacks on Hezbollah, Iran
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the fall of Bashar Assad's regime in Syria is "fraught" with danger, but hailed the development regardless.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The stars will come out at the Kennedy Center for Coppola, the Grateful Dead, Raitt and Sandoval
item.description: Celebrities, cultural icons and a few surprise guests are gathering for the annual Kennedy Center Honors celebration in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Ashraf Khalil, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Clintons urge voters agitated by today's politics to remain involved in public service
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Voters dejected by the presidential election results need to find a way to give back and remain involved, Bill and Hillary Rodham Clinton said Saturday as they celebrated the 20th anniversary of the Clinton presidential library.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massachusetts Democrat says she was targeted by bomb threat
item.description: Rep. Lori Tahan, D-Mass., shared that she and her family were the target of a bomb threat on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Prince William sit down for meeting in Paris after Notre Dame ceremony
item.description: President-elect Trump and Prince William shook hands at the reopening of Notre Dame in Paris on Saturday and later held a sit-down meeting at the British Embassy ahead of his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump is welcomed by Macron to Paris with presidential pomp and joined by Zelenskyy for their talks
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron welcomed Donald Trump to Paris on Saturday with a full dose of presidential pomp as the two held a hastily arranged meeting with Ukraine’s Volodymyr Zelenskyy before celebrating the grand reopening of the Notre Dame Cathedral.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative group compiles list of 'woke' senior officers they want Pete Hegseth to fire
item.description: A conservative research group has compiled a list of 20 “woke" senior officers they want Pete Hegseth to fire should he be confirmed to lead the Pentagon.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As Trump nominee battles brew, NC Senate cleared of raucous onlookers
item.description: Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson cleared the North Carolina Senate chamber this week for the second time in recent days amid protestations against the override of Gov. Cooper's veto on an election board appointments law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Recordings: Woman says slain Kentucky judge was involved in alleged sextortion scandal
item.description: In the recording, a woman claims to have seen video of Mullins engaging in sexual acts in his office.
item.dc:creator: Allison Hope Weiner, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Congressman Torres blasts crime in NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The cold-blooded shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson in the heart of Manhattan shocked New Yorkers, including Congressman Ritchie Torres, leaving the lawmaker to pose the question: “What is happening in New York City?” Torres joined PIX on Politics to discuss city crim
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump taps forceful ally of hard-line immigration policies to head Customs and Border Protection
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The picture of who will be in charge of executing President-elect Donald Trump's hard-line immigration and border policies has come into sharper focus after he announced his picks to head Customs and Border Protection and also the agency tasked with deporting immigrants in the coun
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance tours Helene damage in North Carolina
item.description: The hurricane struck in late September and caused at least $53 billion in damage in North Carolina, according to a state government estimate.
item.dc:creator: Keleigh Beeson, NewsNation

item.title: Biden weighs preemptive pardons before leaving office
item.description: President Joe Biden is weighing sweeping pardons for allies, who the White House believes could be targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s incoming administration, according to people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Vinay Simlot, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: A court upholds the U.S. ban on TikTok
item.description: The appeals court agreed that the viral video app must be sold off by its China-based parent company, rejecting TikTok's claim that the crackdown violates the free speech rights of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: How measles, whooping cough and worse could roar back on RFK Jr.’s watch
item.description: With anti-vaccine activist Robert F. Kennedy Jr. in charge of the nation’s biggest health agency, it wouldn’t take much tampering to enable vaccine-preventable diseases to make a comeback.
item.dc:creator: Arthur Allen, MPR News

item.title: Trump picks Anthony Salisbury as deputy Homeland Security advisor
item.description: Anthony Salisbury is currently a special agent in charge of Homeland Security Investigations (HSI) in Miami.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Donald Trump's incoming team met with Ukraine officials
item.description: Trump's team is trying to bring Ukraine and Russia to the negotiating table quickly, potentially through diplomatic coercion.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Idaho 'abortion trafficking' law will be enforced in part, court rules
item.description: Idaho can enforce a first-of-its-kind "abortion trafficking" law, a federal court ruled, making it illegal for anyone who transports a minor across state lines to get an abortion without parental consent.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: International students confused, anxious about their futures under second Trump term
item.description: International students are holding their breath as President-elect Trump gears up to return to the White House. Multiple schools have put out guidance on when foreign-born students should and shouldn't go abroad to ensure they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Kevin McCarthy: Gaetz lied to Trump before AG nomination
item.description: Should the House Ethics Committee still release its report on Gaetz, even if he’s not going to be part of Trump’s Cabinet?
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota budget update shows $1.1B less than prior forecast
item.description: The economic report released Wednesday morning by state budget officials projects a downturn, with only a $616 million cushion expected by the end of the next budget period. The new data will guide lawmakers when budget deliberations begin in January.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Leadership teams, committee assignments set for 2025 Nevada Legislature
item.description: Leadership positions in the Nevada Legislature are falling into place with less than three months until the 2025 session convenes in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The fight against medical debt is pivoting to the states after Trump's election
item.description: Patient and consumer advocates fear a new Trump administration will scale back federal efforts to expand financial protections for patients and shield them from debt.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: California Assembly Speaker sets new bill limit for legislators
item.description: The California Assembly will limit the number of bills each legislature can introduce from 50 to 35, according to the house rules adopted at the start of the session on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5
